name: healthcare_recall_notice_arrives_stonehaven_auto_ben
premise: A Healthcare clinical command center is in a fast-moving incident involving healthcare recall notice arrives stonehaven auto ben. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is in the next hour. Every delay increases patient safety, triage integrity, and privacy. Stakeholders are already framing this as autonomy vs beneficence under pressure. This decision will be treated as precedent unless you explicitly time-box it. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Ravi Hughes
agents.identity: I am Ravi Hughes, accountable for a defensible call in Healthcare when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays beneficence for autonomy.

agents.name: Leila Diallo
agents.identity: I am Leila Diallo, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Healthcare.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so beneficence isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of autonomy.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect beneficence.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to beneficence to secure autonomy under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is in the next hour. Evidence is partial. Ravi Hughes must decide under autonomy vs beneficence. What does Ravi Hughes do?
